description_long: This is a tall, antique-style bronze or copper vase with a deep green patina, giving it an aged and weathered appearance. The body is bulbous and tapers smoothly toward the base, while the neck flares outward with an irregular, organic rim. A single, thick handle arches from the upper neck to the shoulder, attached with a decorative flourish resembling a stylized leaf or animal motif. The spout is elongated and curves outward, suggesting the vase may have also served as a pitcher or ewer. The surface is mottled with dark green and black patches, indicative of oxidation and long-term exposure to the elements. The overall form and finish evoke a sense of historical craftsmanship, making it suitable as a decorative piece in urban parks, plazas, or courtyards.
description: A tall, antique bronze or copper vase with a green patina, flared rim, and a single ornate handle. Its weathered surface and elongated spout give it a historic, decorative character.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared, irregular rim and the wide, rounded body with a hint of the handle visible on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated spout and the full curve of the handle attached to the neck and shoulder.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle's attachment point and decorative flourish, as well as the vase's broad back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the spout's curve and the handle's arch, with the body tapering toward the base.
